speaker: SHALLOW
text: Nay, you shall see my orchard, where, in an

speaker: SHALLOW
text: arbor, we will eat a last year’s pippin of mine own

speaker: SHALLOW
text: graffing, with a dish of caraways, and so forth.—

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Come, cousin Silence.—And then to bed.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Fore God, you have here a goodly dwelling,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: and a rich.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Barren, barren, barren, beggars all, beggars

speaker: SHALLOW
text: all, Sir John. Marry, good air.—Spread, Davy,

speaker: SHALLOW
text: spread, Davy. Well said, Davy.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: This Davy serves you for good uses. He is

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: your servingman and your husband.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: A good varlet, a good varlet, a very good

speaker: SHALLOW
text: varlet, Sir John. By the Mass, I have drunk too

speaker: SHALLOW
text: much sack at supper. A good varlet. Now sit down,

speaker: SHALLOW
text: now sit down.—Come, cousin.

speaker: SILENCE
text: Ah, sirrah, quoth he, we shall

speaker: SILENCE
text: Do nothing but eat and make good cheer,

speaker: SILENCE
text: And praise God for the merry year,

speaker: SILENCE
text: When flesh is cheap and females dear,

speaker: SILENCE
text: And lusty lads roam here and there

speaker: SILENCE
text: So merrily,

speaker: SILENCE
text: And ever among so merrily.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: There’s a merry heart!—Good Master Silence,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I’ll give you a health for that anon.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Give Master Bardolph some wine, Davy.

speaker: DAVY
text: Sweet sir, sit. I’ll be with you

speaker: DAVY
text: anon. Most sweet sir, sit. Master page, good master

speaker: DAVY
text: page, sit. Proface. What you want in meat, we’ll

speaker: DAVY
text: have in drink, but you must bear. The heart’s all.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Be merry, Master Bardolph.—And, my little

speaker: SHALLOW
text: soldier there, be merry.

speaker: SILENCE
text: Be merry, be merry, my wife has all,

speaker: SILENCE
text: For women are shrews, both short and tall.

speaker: SILENCE
text: ’Tis merry in hall when beards wags all,

speaker: SILENCE
text: And welcome merry Shrovetide.

speaker: SILENCE
text: Be merry, be merry.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I did not think Master Silence had been a

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: man of this mettle.

speaker: SILENCE
text: Who, I? I have been merry twice and once ere

speaker: SILENCE
text: now.

speaker: DAVY
text: There’s a dish of leather-coats for

speaker: DAVY
text: you.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Davy!

speaker: DAVY
text: Your Worship, I’ll be with you straight.—A cup

speaker: DAVY
text: of wine, sir.

speaker: SILENCE
text: A cup of wine that’s brisk and fine,

speaker: SILENCE
text: And drink unto thee, leman mine,

speaker: SILENCE
text: And a merry heart lives long-a.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Well said, Master Silence.

speaker: SILENCE
text: And we shall be merry; now comes in the

speaker: SILENCE
text: sweet o’ th’ night.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Health and long life to you, Master Silence.

speaker: SILENCE
text: Fill the cup, and let it come,

speaker: SILENCE
text: I’ll pledge you a mile to th’ bottom.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Honest Bardolph, welcome. If thou want’st

speaker: SHALLOW
text: anything and wilt not call, beshrew thy heart.—

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Welcome, my little tiny thief, and welcome indeed

speaker: SHALLOW
text: too. I’ll drink to Master Bardolph, and to all the

speaker: SHALLOW
text: cabileros about London.

speaker: DAVY
text: I hope to see London once ere I die.

speaker: BARDOLPH
text: An I might see you there, Davy!

speaker: SHALLOW
text: By the Mass, you’ll crack a quart together,

speaker: SHALLOW
text: ha, will you not, Master Bardolph?

speaker: BARDOLPH
text: Yea, sir, in a pottle-pot.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: By God’s liggens, I thank thee. The knave

speaker: SHALLOW
text: will stick by thee, I can assure thee that. He will not

speaker: SHALLOW
text: out, he. ’Tis true bred!

speaker: BARDOLPH
text: And I’ll stick by him, sir.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Why, there spoke a king. Lack nothing, be

speaker: SHALLOW
text:
merry.
                 
                (
                One
                 
                knocks
                 
                at
                 
                door
                .
                )
               Look who’s at door

speaker: SHALLOW
text: there, ho. Who knocks?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Why, now you have done me right.

speaker: SILENCE
text: Do me right,

speaker: SILENCE
text: And dub me knight,

speaker: SILENCE
text: Samingo.

speaker: SILENCE
text: Is ’t not so?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: ’Tis so.

speaker: SILENCE
text: Is ’t so? Why then, say an old man can do

speaker: SILENCE
text: somewhat.

speaker: DAVY
text: An ’t please your Worship, there’s one Pistol

speaker: DAVY
text: come from the court with news.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: From the court? Let him come in.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: How now, Pistol?

speaker: PISTOL
text: Sir John, God save you.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: What wind blew you hither, Pistol?

speaker: PISTOL
text: Not the ill wind which blows no man to good.

speaker: PISTOL
text: Sweet knight, thou art now one of the greatest men

speaker: PISTOL
text: in this realm.

speaker: SILENCE
text: By ’r Lady, I think he be, but Goodman Puff of

speaker: SILENCE
text: Barson.

speaker: PISTOL
text: Puff?

speaker: PISTOL
text: Puff in thy teeth, most recreant coward base!—

speaker: PISTOL
text: Sir John, I am thy Pistol and thy friend,

speaker: PISTOL
text: And helter-skelter have I rode to thee,

speaker: PISTOL
text: And tidings do I bring, and lucky joys,

speaker: PISTOL
text: And golden times, and happy news of price.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I pray thee now, deliver them like a man of

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: this world.

speaker: PISTOL
text: A foutre for the world and worldlings base!

speaker: PISTOL
text: I speak of Africa and golden joys.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: O base Assyrian knight, what is thy news?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Let King Cophetua know the truth thereof.

speaker: SILENCE
text: And Robin Hood, Scarlet, and John.

speaker: PISTOL
text: Shall dunghill curs confront the Helicons,

speaker: PISTOL
text: And shall good news be baffled?

speaker: PISTOL
text: Then, Pistol, lay thy head in Furies’ lap.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Honest gentleman, I know not your

speaker: SHALLOW
text: breeding.

speaker: PISTOL
text: Why then, lament therefor.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Give me pardon, sir. If, sir, you come with

speaker: SHALLOW
text: news from the court, I take it there’s but two ways,

speaker: SHALLOW
text: either to utter them, or to conceal them. I am, sir,

speaker: SHALLOW
text: under the King in some authority.

speaker: PISTOL
text: Under which king, besonian? Speak or die.

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Under King Harry.

speaker: PISTOL
text: Harry the Fourth, or Fifth?

speaker: SHALLOW
text: Harry the Fourth.

speaker: PISTOL
text: A foutre for thine office!—

speaker: PISTOL
text: Sir John, thy tender lambkin now is king.

speaker: PISTOL
text: Harry the Fifth’s the man. I speak the truth.

speaker: PISTOL
text: When Pistol lies, do this and fig me, like

speaker: PISTOL
text: The bragging Spaniard.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: What, is the old king dead?

speaker: PISTOL
text: As nail in door. The things I speak are just.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Away, Bardolph.—Saddle my horse.—

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Master Robert Shallow, choose what office thou

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: wilt in the land, ’tis thine.—Pistol, I will double-charge

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: thee with dignities.

speaker: BARDOLPH
text: O joyful day! I would not take a knight-hood

speaker: BARDOLPH
text: for my fortune.

speaker: PISTOL
text: What, I do bring good news!

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Carry Master Silence to bed.—Master Shallow,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: my Lord Shallow, be what thou wilt. I am

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Fortune’s steward. Get on thy boots. We’ll ride all

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: night.—O sweet Pistol!—Away, Bardolph!—Come,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Pistol, utter more to me, and withal devise something

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: to do thyself good.—Boot, boot, Master Shallow.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I know the young king is sick for me. Let us

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: take any man’s horses. The laws of England are at

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: my commandment. Blessed are they that have been

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: my friends, and woe to my Lord Chief Justice!

speaker: PISTOL
text: Let vultures vile seize on his lungs also!

speaker: PISTOL
text: “Where is the life that late I led?” say they.

speaker: PISTOL
text: Why, here it is. Welcome these pleasant days.
